fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund

allocations.rationale: Low-cost (0.2% fee), tracks Nifty Midcap 150 (diverse mid-sized stocks), 17%+ annual returns over 3 years.
Balances stocks and bonds (65% equity, 35% debt), 20%+ annual returns over 3 years.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct plan on Groww or Kuvera (no broker fees). Step 2: Start ₹5000/month SIP in both funds. Step 3: Set auto-investment to increase SIP by ₹500/year (e.g., ₹5500 in Year 2, ₹6050 in Year 3, etc.).
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm SIPs are active and no errors.
After 1 year: Check if funds are performing as expected (compare to peers).
Every 3 years: Reassess if goals have changed (e.g., 5L target now closer).
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once/year: If one fund grows much more than planned (e.g., index fund dominates), move 5-10% to the other fund to keep the 70-30 split. No quarterly tweaks: Avoid unnecessary trading; let compounding work.
plan.rationale: Age 22, 38 years ahead: Can afford equity-heavy growth. Midcap stocks (index fund) grow faster than large caps, while aggressive hybrid adds safety. Simple 2-fund plan: No complexity, easy to manage via mobile apps. SIP growth: ₹5000/month grows to ₹5500, ₹6050, etc., compounding over decades. Tax-friendly: Equity-taxed funds save 12.5% on long-term gains (vs 30% slab on debt).
risks: Stock markets drop 20-30% in bad years (e.g., 2008, 2022). Your portfolio value will fall temporarily, but it recovers over time.
Inflation: May erode returns if not matched by fund growth.
No guarantees: Returns depend on market performance, not promises.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

aps.reasoning: The plan has a 70% allocation to a Nifty Midcap 150 index fund (passive) and 30% to an aggressive hybrid fund. The index fund is passive, while the hybrid's activeness is unclear. Rebalancing is annual, minimizing turnover. Cost is emphasized with low fees. Research is not deeply emphasized, relying on index tracking. Long-term horizon with annual reviews. Portfolio activeness is moderate due to index fund and hybrid's potential active management.

pqs.reasoning: The plan aligns well with Remy's moderate risk appetite and long investment horizon, allocating 70% to midcap stocks and 30% to aggressive hybrid. Diversification is good but not perfect, as it focuses on midcap and hybrid funds. Risk-return is appropriate for a 22-year-old with a 38-year horizon. Internal consistency is high, as all elements align with the strategy. Tax efficiency is neutral as the plan uses equity-taxed funds which are favorable for the new regime, but it doesn't explicitly mention tax treatment in detail.
